title: 1-Year Outcomes of Novel Balloon-Expandable vs Contemporary Transcatheter Heart Valves in Severe Aortic Stenosis: The LANDMARK Trial
key_finding: The Myval balloon-expandable transcatheter heart valve demonstrated noninferiority to contemporary SAPIEN and Evolut series valves at 1 year, with comparable freedom from the composite endpoint of death, stroke, and valve-related hospitalizations (87.0% vs 86.9%).
clinical_implications: The Myval THV series provides comparable clinical and hemodynamic outcomes to established transcatheter heart valves, offering an additional treatment option for patients with severe aortic stenosis.

title: The 2025 Accra Declaration on Upskilling and Cost-Effective Cardiac Surgery in Africa
key_finding: The Pan-African Society for Cardiothoracic Surgery established the 2025 Accra Declaration to promote cost-effective cardiac surgery, simulation training, regional databases, and fellowship programs to address cardiovascular disease burden in Africa. The declaration emphasizes specialized working groups for congenital heart surgery, valve surgery, and CABG procedures to improve procedural safety and clinical outcomes.
clinical_implications: This declaration provides a roadmap for developing cardiac surgery infrastructure and training programs across Africa, particularly emphasizing valve surgery as a critical focus area.

title: Genome and transcriptome-wide analyses identify multiple candidate genes and a significant polygenic contribution in bicuspid aortic valve
key_finding: Genome-wide and transcriptome-wide analyses identified multiple candidate genes and demonstrated significant polygenic contribution to bicuspid aortic valve disease. The study revealed genetic factors underlying the most common congenital heart valve abnormality.
clinical_implications: This genetic research advances understanding of bicuspid aortic valve etiology and may lead to improved risk stratification and personalized management strategies.

title: Mitral Annular Disjunction: A Roadmap for the Surgeon
key_finding: Mitral annular disjunction (MAD) is a structural abnormality that increases mechanical stress on mitral leaflets, triggering degenerative processes and associated with mitral valve prolapse and ventricular arrhythmias. Ring annuloplasty is a cornerstone of treatment, stabilizing the annulus and providing antiarrhythmic effects.
clinical_implications: Early surgical intervention may be advocated for patients with MAD and moderate mitral regurgitation in the presence of arrhythmogenic MVP, with ring annuloplasty being essential for stabilizing the annulus.

title: Automated Suture Securing Technology in Mitral Valve Surgery: A Strategy to Reduce Prosthetic Dehiscence?
key_finding: Use of automated titanium fastener device was associated with significantly lower risk of prosthetic dehiscence compared to hand-tied knots in mitral valve surgery (adjusted sHR 0.34, 95% CI 0.12-0.91, P=0.033). No increased risk of stroke, bleeding, or mortality was observed with the automated fastener technology.
clinical_implications: Automated titanium fastener technology may reduce the risk of prosthetic dehiscence in mitral valve surgery by standardizing suture tension and improving mechanical stability of valve implants.

title: Transcatheter edge-to-edge repair vs medical therapy in atrial functional mitral regurgitation: a propensity score-based comparison from the OCEAN-Mitral and REVEAL-AFMR registries
key_finding: TEER for moderate or severe atrial functional mitral regurgitation was associated with lower incidence of all-cause mortality and heart failure hospitalization compared to medical treatment (HR 0.65, 95% CI 0.43-0.99, P=0.044). Favorable outcomes were most pronounced in patients with ≤mild residual AFMR after TEER.
clinical_implications: TEER may be beneficial for high-risk patients with atrial functional mitral regurgitation, particularly when achieving non-significant residual regurgitation.

title: From the Hazards of Death to the Hazards of Indication Creep for TAVR
key_finding: Meta-analysis findings suggesting TAVR mortality advantage over SAVR in lower-risk patients may be influenced by inconsistent risk stratification and limited long-term follow-up data. When excluding intermediate-risk trials, the mortality difference between TAVR and SAVR disappears.
clinical_implications: Advocates for rigorous risk stratification and longer-term data before expanding TAVR indications to avoid inappropriate indication creep in younger, lower-risk populations.

title: Best Practices for Imaging of Transcatheter Valve Failure: An Update From the Heart Valve Collaboratory
key_finding: This updated Heart Valve Collaboratory framework provides standardized pathways and protocols for evaluating transcatheter valve failure using echocardiography and cardiac CT angiography. The document clarifies definitions of bioprosthetic valve deterioration and failure in a practical, imaging-directed context for TAVR.
clinical_implications: This provides standardized imaging protocols for TAVR follow-up, procedural planning for redo-TAVR, and post-redo-TAVR evaluation to optimize lifetime management of aortic valve disease.

title: Transcatheter vs Surgical Aortic Valve Replacement in Lower-Risk Patients: An Updated Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
key_finding: In lower-risk patients at 5 years, TAVR was associated with a 20% reduction in the hazard of all-cause death (HR: 0.80; 95% CI: 0.66-0.97; P = 0.02) and a 19% reduction in death or disabling stroke (HR: 0.81; 95% CI: 0.68-0.96; P = 0.01) compared with SAVR. Stroke rates were equivalent between groups.
clinical_implications: This meta-analysis provides evidence that TAVR may offer survival advantages over SAVR in lower-risk patients with severe aortic stenosis, potentially informing treatment decisions and guidelines for this patient population.

title: Navigating the Landscape of Reinterventions After TAVR
key_finding: TAVR reinterventions remain rare (1.7% at 8 years) but are rapidly increasing, with redo-TAVR accounting for 84% of cases and showing better outcomes than TAVR explant. Most reinterventions (62.1%) occur within the first year.
clinical_implications: Heart teams must carefully select between redo-TAVR and TAVR explant based on mechanism of failure, anatomy, and patient factors, with redo-TAVR generally preferred due to lower mortality rates.

title: Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement in Patients With Systolic Heart Failure and Moderate Aortic Stenosis
key_finding: TAVR was not superior to clinical surveillance for the primary hierarchical composite endpoint in patients with moderate AS and HFrEF, but provided safe and clinically meaningful quality-of-life benefits with greater improvement in KCCQ scores at 1 year.
clinical_implications: Preemptive TAVR for moderate AS in HFrEF patients may offer quality-of-life benefits but does not significantly improve the composite clinical endpoint compared to surveillance with intervention upon progression to severe AS.

title: Surgical ablation of atrial fibrillation with concomitant cardiac surgery: a state-of-the-art review
key_finding: Concomitant surgical ablation, particularly the Cox-maze IV procedure, significantly improves sinus rhythm restoration, reduces mortality and mitigates complications like stroke in cardiac surgery patients with preoperative atrial fibrillation. However, the procedure remains underperformed with performance rates varying between 22% and 48% depending on the type of procedure.
clinical_implications: Surgical ablation should be more widely implemented during cardiac surgery in patients with atrial fibrillation, as it can improve survival and reduce AF-related complications despite current underutilization due to concerns about complexity and operative time.

title: Infective endocarditis: it takes a team
key_finding: A multi-disciplinary endocarditis team-based approach has become the standard of care for infective endocarditis management, with multiple investigations showing potentially beneficial patient outcomes. The epidemiology has shifted to involve older patients with prosthetic devices, multiple comorbidities, and increasingly virulent pathogens.
clinical_implications: Multi-disciplinary endocarditis teams should be implemented as standard of care to provide coordinated, standardized, and comprehensive management of this complex disease affecting multiple organ systems.

title: Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement With Balloon- Versus Self-Expandable Bioprostheses for the Treatment of Bicuspid Aortic Valve Stenosis
key_finding: In patients with bicuspid aortic valve stenosis undergoing TAVR, death or stroke did not significantly differ between balloon-expandable and self-expandable valves over 3 years, though device-specific complications varied.
clinical_implications: Both balloon-expandable and self-expandable TAVR devices show similar clinical outcomes for bicuspid aortic valve stenosis, but procedural considerations should account for device-specific complication profiles.

title: Cardiac Biomarkers in Patients With Asymptomatic Severe Aortic Stenosis: Analysis From the EARLY TAVR Trial
key_finding: Higher NT-proBNP and hs-cTnT levels were associated with higher event rates in asymptomatic severe aortic stenosis, but the relative benefit of early TAVR was consistent regardless of baseline biomarker levels and tended to be more pronounced in patients with the lowest biomarker levels.
clinical_implications: These findings suggest limited value for single measurements of NT-proBNP and hs-cTnT to guide the timing of TAVR in asymptomatic patients with severe aortic stenosis.

title: Conduction Disturbances and Outcome After Surgical Aortic Valve Replacement in Patients With Bicuspid and Tricuspid Aortic Stenosis
key_finding: BAV-AS patients had higher rates of new-onset third-degree AV block (6.5% vs 2.5%) and LBBB (9.7% vs 5.7%) after SAVR compared to TAV-AS patients, with new-onset LBBB associated with increased mortality (HR 1.60). BAV patients with fusion of right- and non-coronary cusps had the highest risk for conduction disturbances.
clinical_implications: Bicuspid aortic valve patients, especially those with right-non-coronary cusp fusion, require heightened monitoring for conduction disturbances after SAVR given their increased risk and associated mortality implications.

title: Long-term clinical outcomes in patients between the age of 50–70 years receiving biological versus mechanical aortic valve prostheses
key_finding: Patients aged 50-70 years with mechanical aortic prostheses had better long-term survival compared to those with biological prostheses, with size 19-mm biological valves showing the worst outcomes and severe patient-prosthesis mismatch associated with lowest survival.
clinical_implications: This study suggests a need for critical re-evaluation of prosthesis selection strategies in the 50-70 age group, potentially favoring mechanical valves for improved long-term survival.

title: Percutaneous Cannulation for Minimally Invasive Heart Valve Surgery: Results from a Multicenter Registry
key_finding: Both plug-based and suture-based vascular closure devices had low vascular access site complication rates (2.9% vs 5.2%), but plug-based devices achieved significantly higher immediate hemostasis rates (99.8% vs 77.7%) and required fewer conversions to surgical cut-down.
clinical_implications: Percutaneous cannulation with dedicated vascular closure devices is safe and feasible for minimally invasive heart valve surgery, with plug-based devices potentially offering superior immediate hemostasis.

title: Deep learning for echocardiographic assessment and risk stratification of aortic, mitral, and tricuspid regurgitation: the DELINEATE-regurgitation study
key_finding: An AI system using multiview echocardiographic approaches achieved substantial agreement with cardiologist interpretation for classifying AR, MR, and TR severity (weighted kappa 0.81/0.76, 0.76/0.72, and 0.73/0.64 respectively). A separate AI model successfully predicted MR progression risk beyond currently known clinical factors with hazard ratio 4.1.
clinical_implications: AI systems can improve accuracy and reproducibility of valve regurgitation assessment and help identify patients with mild-moderate MR who are most likely to progress and benefit from intensive monitoring.

title: Tricuspid regurgitation and outcomes in mitral valve transcatheter edge-to-edge repair
key_finding: Post-procedural tricuspid regurgitation, but not baseline TR, was associated with outcomes after mitral transcatheter edge-to-edge repair, with new-onset TR having the highest risk for cardiovascular death or heart failure hospitalization.
clinical_implications: Careful tricuspid regurgitation assessment after mitral TEER would provide optimal management for concomitant significant TR in patients undergoing the procedure.

title: Cardiac transthyretin amyloidosis treatment improves outcomes after aortic valve replacement for severe stenosis
key_finding: ATTR-specific medication (99% tafamidis) was independently associated with lower all-cause mortality (HR 0.40) and cardiovascular mortality (HR 0.47) in patients with dual aortic stenosis and cardiac amyloidosis. Patients receiving both ATTR-specific treatment and AVR had the most favorable prognosis.
clinical_implications: Both ATTR-specific therapy and aortic valve replacement provide significant survival benefits in patients with concomitant aortic stenosis and transthyretin cardiac amyloidosis, with optimal outcomes when both treatments are combined.

title: Permanent pacemaker implantation following mitral valve surgery. State-of-the-art scoping review
key_finding: The incidence of permanent pacemaker implantation after isolated mitral valve surgery varies from 1% to 10%, with atrioventricular block being the most common indication and long-term pacemaker dependency ranging between 20% and 60%.
clinical_implications: This review highlights the need for standardized approaches to identify patients requiring permanent pacemakers after mitral valve surgery and strategies to reduce postoperative conduction abnormalities.

title: Surgical decision-making for concomitant tricuspid valve repair in minimally invasive mitral valve surgery
key_finding: Among patients undergoing minimally invasive mitral valve surgery with indications for tricuspid valve repair, 167 patients (15.5%) did not receive concomitant tricuspid repair, with decisions influenced by severity of tricuspid regurgitation, NYHA class, and institutional practices.
clinical_implications: Clinical and institutional factors strongly influence adherence to guideline recommendations for concomitant tricuspid valve repair, suggesting need for standardized treatment approaches to improve outcomes.

title: The First International Consortium for Health Outcomes Measurement (ICHOM) Standard Dataset for Reporting Outcomes in Heart Valve Disease: Moving From Device- to Patient-Centered Outcomes
key_finding: A multisociety working group developed a standardized dataset with 16 outcome measures including patient-reported outcomes for heart valve disease, moving from procedure-focused to patient-centered outcome measurement. The standard set includes 5 outcome domains covering vital status, patient-reported outcomes, disease progression, cardiac function/durability, and treatment complications.
clinical_implications: Implementation of this standardized dataset will facilitate global comparison of heart valve disease outcomes and improve patient care worldwide by focusing on patient-centered rather than device-centered outcomes.

title: Reply to Xiao et al.
key_finding: The authors respond to commentary on the PERIGON trial, acknowledging limitations in traditional SVD definitions and reporting that endocarditis rates were not significantly different between patients ≤65 and >65 years of age.
clinical_implications: Reliable and standardized methods to identify structural valve deterioration are still needed for optimal valve selection in patients.

title: Seven-year outcomes after surgical aortic valve replacement with a stented bovine pericardial bioprosthesis in over 1100 patients: a prospective multicentre analysis
key_finding: The Avalus valve demonstrated excellent 7-year durability with a freedom from structural valve deterioration/severe hemodynamic dysfunction rate of 98.8% and no cases adjudicated as SVD. Survival rate was 82.6% with stable hemodynamic performance.
clinical_implications: This long-term follow-up supports the Avalus valve as a durable option for surgical aortic valve replacement with excellent clinical outcomes through 7 years.

title: Surgical Bailout in Patients Undergoing Transfemoral Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement: Incidence, Trends, and Clinical Outcomes
key_finding: Surgical bailout occurred in 0.52% of transfemoral TAVR patients and decreased over time from 0.84% in 2007-2010 to 0.25% in 2019-2022, with 1-year mortality remaining high at 68.3% versus 15.3% in patients without bailout.
clinical_implications: The very low incidence of surgical bailout in contemporary TAVR practice supports the safety of the procedure while emphasizing the continued need for surgical backup given the high mortality risk when bailout is required.

title: Tricuspid Regurgitation Disease Stages and Treatment Outcomes After Transcatheter Tricuspid Valve Repair
key_finding: T-TEER was associated with improved 1-year survival only in patients with intermediate disease stage (HR: 0.73), but not in early or advanced disease stages when compared to medical management.
clinical_implications: The timing of T-TEER intervention based on disease stage is crucial for optimizing treatment benefits, suggesting intermediate disease stage may be the optimal window for intervention.

title: Impact of Pulmonary Hypertension on Outcomes After Transcatheter Tricuspid Valve Edge-to-Edge Repair
key_finding: Increasing systolic pulmonary artery pressure was independently associated with higher rates of 2-year death or heart failure hospitalization (HR 1.027) after T-TEER, with an optimal threshold of 46 mmHg for predicting adverse outcomes. NYHA functional class improved and tricuspid regurgitation severity was reduced regardless of pulmonary hypertension status.
clinical_implications: Pulmonary hypertension is an important outcome predictor in T-TEER patients, but the procedure remains beneficial for symptom relief and TR reduction even in patients with elevated pulmonary pressures.

title: Long-Term Outcomes of Patients Requiring Pacemaker Implantation After Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement
key_finding: Patients requiring pacemaker implantation within 30 days after TAVR had significantly higher all-cause mortality (aHR: 1.15) and cardiovascular mortality (aHR: 1.25) at 1-year follow-up, with trends persisting at 5- and 10-year follow-up.
clinical_implications: Pacemaker implantation after TAVR is associated with increased long-term mortality risk, which is important for patient counseling and follow-up management as TAVR expands to lower-risk and younger patients.

title: Transcatheter Edge-to-Edge Repair in Patients With Primary Tricuspid Regurgitation
key_finding: TEER was successfully performed in 95.6% of patients with primary TR, achieving TR reduction to moderate or less in 83.3% with 1.8% in-hospital mortality. At 1 year, 79.7% maintained TR moderate or less with significant improvements in right heart parameters and functional class.
clinical_implications: TEER is a safe and effective alternative to surgery for selected patients with primary tricuspid regurgitation, promoting reverse remodeling and symptomatic relief.

title: Continuation versus Interruption of Oral Anticoagulation during TAVI
key_finding: Periprocedural continuation of oral anticoagulation was not noninferior to interruption during TAVI, with higher bleeding rates in the continuation group (31.1% vs 21.3%) but similar thromboembolic events (8.8% vs 8.2%).
clinical_implications: The results suggest that interruption of oral anticoagulation may be preferred during TAVI procedures to reduce bleeding risk without increasing thromboembolic complications.

title: Early Intervention in Patients with Asymptomatic Severe Aortic Stenosis and Myocardial Fibrosis: The EVOLVED Randomized Clinical Trial
key_finding: The EVOLVED trial compared early aortic valve intervention versus guideline-directed conservative management in asymptomatic patients with severe aortic stenosis and myocardial fibrosis.
clinical_implications: This study addresses the timing of intervention in asymptomatic severe aortic stenosis patients with evidence of myocardial fibrosis, potentially informing earlier intervention strategies.

title: Hospitalization of Symptomatic Patients With Heart Failure and Moderate to Severe Functional Mitral Regurgitation Treated With MitraClip: Insights From RESHAPE-HF2
key_finding: MitraClip transcatheter edge-to-edge repair reduced cardiovascular death or heart failure hospitalizations (HR 0.65) and recurrent cardiovascular hospitalizations (RR 0.75) at 24 months in patients with moderate to severe functional mitral regurgitation and symptomatic heart failure.
clinical_implications: This analysis supports the use of MitraClip in heart failure patients with functional mitral regurgitation to reduce hospitalizations and improve outcomes.

title: LANDMARK comparison of early outcomes of newer-generation Myval transcatheter heart valve series with contemporary valves (Sapien and Evolut) in real-world individuals with severe symptomatic native aortic stenosis: a randomised non-inferiority trial
key_finding: The Myval transcatheter heart valve demonstrated non-inferiority to contemporary valves (Sapien and Evolut) with a 25% vs 27% rate of the primary composite endpoint at 30 days, meeting the prespecified non-inferiority margin (risk difference -2.3%, p<0.0001).
clinical_implications: The Myval THV with 1.5mm incremental sizing provides a clinically acceptable alternative to established TAVR devices for treating severe aortic stenosis.

title: TransCatheter aortic valve implantation and fractional flow reserve-guided percutaneous coronary intervention versus conventional surgical aortic valve replacement and coronary bypass grafting for treatment of patients with aortic valve stenosis and complex or multivessel coronary disease (TCW)
key_finding: FFR-guided PCI plus TAVI was non-inferior and superior to SAVR plus CABG for the primary composite endpoint (4% vs 23%), driven mainly by lower all-cause mortality (0% vs 10%) and life-threatening bleeding (2% vs 12%). This is the first trial comparing percutaneous versus surgical treatment in patients with severe aortic stenosis and complex coronary disease.
clinical_implications: This trial supports considering TAVI plus FFR-guided PCI as a preferred treatment option over combined SAVR and CABG in elderly patients with severe aortic stenosis and complex coronary disease.

title: Axillary vs Femoral Arterial Cannulation in Acute Type A Dissection: International Multicenter Data
key_finding: Both axillary and femoral arterial cannulation strategies showed equivalent early mortality (15% vs 14%), stroke, tamponade, and malperfusion outcomes in acute type A aortic dissection repair. Axillary cannulation was associated with more extensive procedures including valve-sparing root replacements.
clinical_implications: Surgeons can choose either axillary or femoral cannulation approach for acute type A dissection repair as both have similar safety profiles, though axillary cannulation may facilitate more complex valve-sparing procedures.

title: Conflicts of interest in clinical practice: lessons learned from cardiovascular medicine
key_finding: Conflicts of interest may influence cardiovascular research at multiple levels, from research question formulation through data collection to result reporting, particularly affecting large randomized controlled trials that influence international guidelines. Despite specific protocols to mitigate COI influence, even scientific societies and guideline panels may not be totally free from COIs.
clinical_implications: Managing conflicts of interest effectively is essential to preserve public trust and evidence-based medicine in cardiovascular practice, particularly for valve therapies and interventional procedures.

title: Patient-prosthesis mismatch after surgical aortic valve replacement: analysis of the PARTNER trials
key_finding: Severe patient-prosthesis mismatch by predicted method occurred in only 2.1% of SAVR patients, while the measured method overestimated severe PPM incidence but was associated with worse outcomes. Severe PPM was associated with higher all-cause mortality and heart failure rehospitalization at 2 years.
clinical_implications: Surgical techniques to minimize patient-prosthesis mismatch remain critical given the association between severe PPM and worse clinical outcomes after SAVR.

title: A Nationwide Study on Mitral Valve Repair vs Replacement for Active Endocarditis
key_finding: Mitral valve repair demonstrated superior outcomes compared to replacement in active infective endocarditis with an adjusted hazard ratio of 2.216 (95% CI, 1.425-3.448; P < .001) for replacement. Early mortality was 13.0% and repair was feasible in 41.1% of patients.
clinical_implications: These results support mitral valve repair over replacement when feasible in active infective endocarditis, though repair rates are declining despite improved outcomes.

title: Contemporary Outcomes and Trends for the Transseptal Mitral Valve-in-Valve Procedure Using Balloon Expandable Transcatheter Valves in the United States
key_finding: Transseptal mitral valve-in-valve procedures demonstrated high technical success (96.6%) with acceptable mortality rates (3.2% in-hospital, 13.4% at 1-year) and significant improvements in functional class and quality of life.
clinical_implications: Mitral valve-in-valve is a reasonable therapy for patients with degenerated bioprosthetic mitral valves who are anatomical candidates, with particularly excellent outcomes in low-risk and intermediate-risk patients.

title: Rare Genetic Variants in LDLR, APOB, and PCSK9 Are Associated With Aortic Stenosis
key_finding: Rare protein-disrupting variants in LDLR increased aortic stenosis risk (OR 3.52), while variants in APOB or PCSK9 decreased risk (OR 0.49), with corresponding effects on LDL-C levels and peak aortic valve velocities.
clinical_implications: Early and sustained lipid-lowering therapy may prevent or slow aortic stenosis development, particularly when initiated before advanced disease stages.

title: Tricuspid valve disease and cardiac implantable electronic devices
key_finding: CIED-related tricuspid regurgitation is an independent clinical entity where TR worsens by at least 1 grade in about 20% of patients after CIED implantation, with severe lead-related TR occurring in 4-7% of cases.
clinical_implications: This multidisciplinary review provides clinical guidance for diagnosis and treatment algorithms for patients with TR associated with or related to cardiac implantable electronic devices.

title: Transcatheter or surgical aortic valve implantation: 10-year outcomes of the NOTION trial
key_finding: At 10 years, TAVI and SAVR showed similar rates of major clinical outcomes (all-cause mortality, stroke, or MI: 65.5% vs 65.5%), but TAVI had lower rates of severe structural valve deterioration (1.5% vs 10.0%).
clinical_implications: Long-term outcomes support TAVI as a durable option comparable to SAVR in lower surgical risk patients with severe aortic stenosis.

title: 20 Years of triple-valve surgery in the UK: demographic and outcome trends
key_finding: In-hospital mortality for triple-valve surgery improved from 21% to 7% over 20 years (2000-2019), while mean patient age increased from 63 to 69 years. Triple valve replacement, redo sternotomy, and poor left ventricular function were strong predictors of mortality.
clinical_implications: Triple-valve surgery outcomes have improved significantly over time, and attempts should be made to repair mitral and/or tricuspid valves when technically feasible rather than replace them.

title: Tissue versus mechanical mitral valve replacement in patients aged 50–70: a propensity-matched analysis
key_finding: Survival following mechanical and bioprosthetic mitral valve replacement in patients aged 50-70 years is similar up to 15 years of follow-up, but bioprosthetic valves are associated with increased risk of repeat MVR.
clinical_implications: Valve selection in this intermediate age group requires careful consideration of structural valve deterioration risk versus bleeding and thromboembolic complications.

title: Simultaneous versus staged approach in transcatheter aortic valve implantation for severe stenosis and endovascular aortic repair for thoracic and abdominal aortic aneurysm
key_finding: Both simultaneous and staged TAVI with T/EVAR procedures are effective, but simultaneous repair reduces length of stay (7 vs 19 days) and pulmonary complications while maintaining similar 3-year survival rates (79%).
clinical_implications: Simultaneous TAVI and endovascular aortic repair may be preferred over staged approaches due to shorter hospitalization and fewer complications without compromising survival outcomes.

title: Statin treatment after surgical aortic valve replacement for aortic stenosis is associated with better long-term outcome
key_finding: Ongoing statin treatment after SAVR was associated with reduced risk for major adverse cardiovascular events (aHR 0.77, 95% CI 0.71-0.83), mainly driven by reduction in all-cause mortality (aHR 0.70, 95% CI 0.64-0.76).
clinical_implications: Results suggest statin therapy may be beneficial for patients undergoing surgical aortic valve replacement for aortic stenosis, though randomized controlled trials are needed to establish causality.

title: Long-term outcomes of isolated mechanical versus bioprosthetic mitral valve replacement in different age groups of propensity-matched patients
key_finding: In patients <65 years, mechanical mitral valves showed superior 10-year survival (78.2% vs 69.8%) and freedom from reintervention (96.2% vs 81.3%) compared to bioprosthetic valves, while no differences were observed in patients aged 65-75 years.
clinical_implications: This supports current guidelines recommending mechanical valves for patients <65 years and suggests no clear advantage for either valve type in the 65-75 year age group for isolated mitral valve replacement.

title: Long-term results of edge-to-edge and neochordal mitral repair for isolated anterior leaflet lesion: a propensity match analysis
key_finding: Edge-to-edge repair showed superior long-term durability compared to neochordal repair for isolated anterior mitral leaflet lesions, with 20-year freedom from reoperation of 78% vs 64% and lower mitral regurgitation recurrence rates.
clinical_implications: Edge-to-edge technique should be considered the preferred approach for isolated anterior mitral leaflet pathology given its superior durability, simplicity, and reproducibility up to 20 years follow-up.

title: Mitral transcatheter edge to edge repair versus isolated mitral surgery for severe mitral regurgitation: A French nationwide study
key_finding: TEER was associated with significantly lower cardiovascular death (HR 0.685) and lower rates of pacemaker implantation and stroke compared to mitral surgery at 3-year follow-up. However, non-cardiovascular death was more frequent after TEER, with no significant difference in all-cause death.
clinical_implications: TEER may be preferred over surgery for severe mitral regurgitation in appropriately selected patients given lower cardiovascular mortality and reduced procedural complications.

title: Benefit of isolated surgical valve repair or replacement for functional tricuspid regurgitation and long-term outcomes stratified by the TRI-SCORE
key_finding: This study from the TRIGISTRY international cohort examined survival outcomes up to 10 years in patients with severe isolated functional tricuspid regurgitation, comparing surgical repair/replacement to conservative management stratified by TRI-SCORE. The study aimed to identify patient subsets who might benefit from tricuspid valve surgery.
clinical_implications: This research helps identify optimal timing and patient selection for isolated tricuspid valve surgery in functional tricuspid regurgitation using the TRI-SCORE risk stratification tool.

title: Leaflet modification before transcatheter aortic valve implantation in patients at risk for coronary obstruction: the ShortCut study
key_finding: The ShortCut device achieved successful leaflet splitting in all patients (100%) undergoing TAVI with failed bioprosthetic aortic valves at risk for coronary obstruction, with 98.3% freedom from procedure-related mortality or stroke at discharge. At 30 days, freedom from coronary obstruction was 95%.
clinical_implications: Leaflet modification using the ShortCut device provides a safe and effective method to prevent coronary obstruction during valve-in-valve TAVI procedures.

title: A streamlined pathway for transcatheter aortic valve implantation: the BENCHMARK study
key_finding: Implementation of BENCHMARK best practices for TAVI significantly reduced hospital length of stay from 7.7 to 5.8 days (median 6 vs. 4 days) and intensive care duration while maintaining patient safety across 28 European sites.
clinical_implications: Streamlined TAVI care pathways can improve hospital efficiency and reduce resource utilization without compromising patient safety outcomes.

title: Atrial secondary tricuspid regurgitation: pathophysiology, definition, diagnosis, and treatment
key_finding: Atrial secondary tricuspid regurgitation (A-STR) is a distinct phenotype of secondary TR characterized by predominant right atrial dilation with preserved ventricular function, occurring commonly in elderly women with atrial fibrillation. A-STR accounts for 10-15% of clinically relevant TR and has better outcomes than ventricular phenotypes.
clinical_implications: Patients with A-STR may benefit from more aggressive rhythm control and timely valve interventions, requiring distinct evaluation and management approaches compared to ventricular secondary TR.

title: Selection for transcatheter versus surgical aortic valve replacement and mid-term survival: results of the AUTHEARTVISIT study
key_finding: In this population-based study of 18,882 patients, TAVR was associated with significantly higher all-cause mortality compared to surgical bioprosthetic AVR (HR 1.552, 95% CI 1.469-1.640, P<0.001), with estimated median survival of 5 years vs 8.8 years respectively. The survival benefit for surgical AVR persisted after propensity score matching and in subgroup analyses of patients aged 65-75 years and >75 years.
clinical_implications: This real-world evidence suggests that patient selection for TAVR versus surgical AVR significantly impacts long-term survival, with surgical AVR showing superior mid-term outcomes even in older patient populations.

title: Examining the typical hemodynamic performance of nearly 3000 modern surgical aortic bioprostheses
key_finding: Pooled analysis of 2958 patients with contemporary surgical aortic valves showed good to excellent hemodynamic performance at 1 year, with effective orifice area ranging from 1.46-2.12 cm2 and mean gradients from 8.6-16.1 mmHg.
clinical_implications: These reference values from the largest core lab-assessed cohort of contemporary surgical aortic valves provide benchmarks for clinicians to evaluate normal valve performance and detect structural valve deterioration over time.

title: On-table extubation is associated with reduced intensive care unit stay and hospitalization after trans-axillary minimally invasive mitral valve surgery
key_finding: Fast track extubation was achievable in 79% of patients undergoing trans-axillary mitral valve surgery, with on-table extubation associated with shorter ICU stays and reduced hospitalization compared to delayed extubation.
clinical_implications: Early extubation protocols in minimally invasive mitral valve surgery are safe and can reduce ICU length of stay and overall hospitalization without increasing complications.

title: Long-term surgical outcomes of congenital supravalvular aortic stenosis: a systematic review, meta-analysis and microsimulation study
key_finding: After surgical repair for congenital supravalvular aortic stenosis, pooled early mortality was 4.2% and patients had an observed 30-year life expectancy that was 90.7% of the matched general population, with 31.3% risk of reintervention over 30 years.
clinical_implications: Lifelong cardiovascular monitoring is recommended for SVAS patients given the considerable lifetime risk of reintervention and reduced survival compared to the general population.

title: Five-year outcomes of different techniques for minimally invasive mitral valve repair in Barlow's disease
key_finding: Isolated annuloplasty, use of additional artificial chordae, and leaflet resection showed comparable 5-year outcomes for minimally invasive mitral valve repair in Barlow's disease, though isolated annuloplasty had higher conversion rates to valve replacement.
clinical_implications: The pathology of Barlow's disease should not be oversimplified with isolated annuloplasty given the increased conversion rate, suggesting more complex repair techniques may be needed.

title: Learning curve of robotic mitral repair: prospective two-centre study of proficiency and clinical outcomes
key_finding: A successful robotic-assisted mitral repair programme can be safely started from either conventional open approach or mini thoracotomy MIMVS, with the learning curve ending at the 60th case in centre 1 and 50th case in centre 2. Previous mini thoracotomy MIMVS experience had positive impact on the initial learning curve, with shorter surgical times and faster skill acquisition.
clinical_implications: Previous minimally invasive mitral valve surgery experience facilitates the transition to robotic-assisted mitral repair with shorter operative times and steeper learning curves.

title: Percutaneous Edge-to-Edge Repair for Tricuspid Regurgitation: 3-Year Outcomes From the TRILUMINATE Study
key_finding: At 3 years, tricuspid transcatheter edge-to-edge repair with TriClip reduced TR to moderate or less in 79% of subjects and achieved at least 1 grade reduction in 92% of subjects. Heart failure hospitalization rates decreased by 75% from 0.56 to 0.14 events/patient-year.
clinical_implications: The TriClip procedure demonstrates sustained safety and efficacy at 3 years with durable TR reduction and improved clinical outcomes for patients with symptomatic tricuspid regurgitation.

title: Prognostic Implications of Residual Tricuspid Regurgitation Grading After Transcatheter Tricuspid Valve Repair
key_finding: Residual tricuspid regurgitation grade following transcatheter tricuspid valve interventions significantly impacts patient survival. Procedural success should be defined as achieving mild-to-moderate or lower tricuspid regurgitation grade at discharge.
clinical_implications: This study provides evidence for redefining procedural success criteria in transcatheter tricuspid valve repair based on residual regurgitation severity rather than just device deployment success.

title: Pulmonary Valve Replacement in Tetralogy of Fallot: Procedural Volume and Durability of Bioprosthetic Pulmonary Valves
key_finding: The annual number of pulmonary valve replacements increased from 0.4 to 6.0 per million population from 1976-2021, with median bioprosthetic valve durability of 17 years. Younger age at PVR and smaller valve diameter predicted reduced durability.
clinical_implications: These findings support consideration of valve size and patient age when planning timing of pulmonary valve replacement in tetralogy of Fallot patients.

title: Transapical Mitral Valve Replacement: 1-Year Results of the Real-World Tendyne European Experience Registry
key_finding: Tendyne TMVR achieved 95% technical success, 98% durable MR reduction to ≤1+, and 17% 1-year cardiovascular mortality in a real-world registry of 195 patients. Outcomes were similar between on-label and real-world indications including severe MAC.
clinical_implications: Tendyne TMVR offers a safe and effective treatment option for high-risk mitral valve disease patients, including those with severe MAC previously excluded from trials.

title: The Impact of Cerebral Embolic Protection Devices on Characteristics and Outcomes of Stroke Complicating TAVR
key_finding: In 18,725 TAVR procedures with 416 acute ischemic strokes (2.2%), cerebral embolic protection devices had little effect on stroke distribution, severity, or outcomes compared to unprotected TAVR.
clinical_implications: The use of cerebral embolic protection devices during TAVR does not appear to significantly reduce stroke severity or improve outcomes when stroke does occur.

title: Transjugular Transcatheter Tricuspid Valve Replacement: Early Compassionate Use Outcomes
key_finding: Transjugular TTVR with the LuX-Valve Plus system achieved effective TR reduction to ≤2+ in 94.7% and ≤1+ in 90.8% of patients, with 5.3% in-hospital mortality and 94.4% survival at 1-month. Outcomes were comparable between patients receiving large devices (≥55 mm) and smaller sizes.
clinical_implications: Transjugular TTVR appears to be a safe and effective treatment option for patients with severe tricuspid regurgitation, including those with very large tricuspid anatomies requiring devices ≥55 mm.

title: Incidence, Predictors, and Outcomes of Paravalvular Regurgitation After TAVR in Sievers Type 1 Bicuspid Aortic Valves
key_finding: Moderate or severe paravalvular regurgitation occurred in 3.8% of patients with Sievers type 1 bicuspid aortic valve stenosis undergoing TAVR with current-generation devices and was associated with a 2.52-fold increased risk of major adverse events. Independent predictors included larger virtual raphe ring perimeter, severe calcification, self-expanding valves, and intentional supra-annular positioning.
clinical_implications: TAVR in bicuspid aortic valves requires careful patient selection and procedural planning to minimize paravalvular regurgitation risk, particularly avoiding self-expanding valves and supra-annular positioning in high-risk anatomies.

title: Hydrodynamic Assessment of Explanted Degenerated Transcatheter Aortic Valves: Novel Insights Into Noncalcific and Calcific Mechanisms
key_finding: TAV function can be severely impacted by both noncalcific and calcific mechanisms of tissue degeneration, with functional stenosis occurring even in the absence of significant calcification.
clinical_implications: This finding suggests that mechanisms beyond calcification contribute to TAV degeneration and may influence treatment selection and timing of reintervention.

title: Cardiac damage and quality of life after aortic valve replacement in the PARTNER trials
key_finding: The extent of extravalvular cardiac damage at baseline was associated with worse quality of life before and after AVR, and each 1-stage increase in cardiac damage was associated with a 24% increase in the odds of poor outcomes at 1 year.
clinical_implications: Earlier detection and treatment of aortic stenosis before irreversible cardiac damage develops may improve health status outcomes after valve replacement.

title: Tricuspid Valve Academic Research Consortium Definitions for Tricuspid Regurgitation and Trial Endpoints
key_finding: This first TVARC document standardizes definitions of tricuspid regurgitation disease etiology, severity, and trial endpoints to address knowledge gaps in patient identification and management. The consortium aims to provide consistency across clinical trials and strengthen evidence for future guideline recommendations.
clinical_implications: Standardized definitions and trial endpoints should enable more meaningful comparisons between tricuspid valve clinical trials and improve evidence generation for treatment recommendations.

title: Tricuspid Valve Academic Research Consortium Definitions for Tricuspid Regurgitation and Trial Endpoints
key_finding: The TVARC consortium established standardized definitions for tricuspid regurgitation disease etiology, severity assessment, and clinical trial endpoints to enable meaningful comparisons between studies. The document addresses knowledge gaps in patient identification and management of tricuspid regurgitation.
clinical_implications: These standardized definitions will improve consistency in tricuspid valve research and clinical trials, potentially leading to better patient selection and treatment strategies.

title: The MIDA-Q Mortality Risk Score: A Quantitative Prognostic Tool for the Mitral Valve Prolapse Spectrum
key_finding: The MIDA-Q score (range 0-15) demonstrates strong, independent association with long-term survival after MVP diagnosis, with 5-year survival ranging from 97% (score 0) to 5% (score ≥13). The score provides incremental prognostic power beyond conventional risk scores across the entire MVP spectrum.
clinical_implications: The MIDA-Q score provides crucial risk stratification for all MVP patients regardless of DMR severity, enabling better clinical management and trial design beyond the current binary focus on severe DMR.

title: 2023 ESC Guidelines for the management of endocarditis
key_finding: Updated comprehensive European guidelines for the prevention, diagnosis, and management of infective endocarditis with emphasis on multidisciplinary endocarditis team approach.
clinical_implications: Provides evidence-based recommendations for standardized care of endocarditis patients including prevention strategies, diagnostic approaches, and treatment protocols.

title: Aortic valve replacement in non-elderly: the gap between reality, guidelines and evidence
key_finding: Current practice patterns for aortic valve replacement in non-elderly patients (18-60 years) vary widely among clinicians, with mechanical valves being most frequently used, though patient involvement in decision-making occurs in only 77% of cases.
clinical_implications: There is need for improved collaboration between cardiac departments and better education to enable shared decision-making for young patients requiring aortic valve replacement.

title: Transcatheter tricuspid valve edge-to-edge repair after a heart transplant: a single-centre experience with a novel therapy
key_finding: T-TEER using MitraClip G4 device in TriClip guiding sheath was feasible in 7 heart transplant patients with severe TR, achieving technical success in 6/7 patients with significant TR reduction and right ventricular remodeling. Median time from transplant to T-TEER was 13 years, with durable TR reduction maintained at 10 months median follow-up.
clinical_implications: T-TEER appears to be a safer alternative to high-risk surgical tricuspid valve replacement in heart transplant recipients with severe tricuspid regurgitation.

title: A mitral valve repair update: simple, intuitive and physiological techniques, using running sutures (part I)
key_finding: The author proposes new mitral valve repair techniques using running sutures through pathological leaflet margins that follow standard anatomical landmarks to reduce inter-surgeon variability and simplify the procedure. Early experience with 8 cases showed excellent immediate outcomes with only trace or zero residual regurgitation.
clinical_implications: These standardized techniques may enhance adoption of mitral valve repair by surgeons with average experience and reduce variability in outcomes between different surgeons.

title: Repairable ex vivo model of functional and degenerative mitral regurgitation
key_finding: Novel ex vivo models of functional and degenerative mitral regurgitation were successfully developed with controllable regurgitation severity (mean regurgitant fraction 47.9% and 58.5% respectively), which were significantly reduced after repair procedures (28.7% and 26.0% respectively).
clinical_implications: These reproducible ex vivo models could expedite the development and preclinical evaluation of novel transcatheter mitral valve repair devices.

title: Medical therapy after surgical aortic valve replacement for aortic regurgitation
key_finding: Treatment with RAS inhibitors and statins was associated with reduced risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (aHR 0.71 and 0.78 respectively), while β-blocker treatment was associated with increased risk (aHR 1.35).
clinical_implications: RAS inhibitors and statins may be beneficial as secondary preventive therapy after surgical aortic valve replacement for aortic regurgitation.

title: Outcomes of transcatheter aortic valve replacement in patients with cardiogenic shock
key_finding: TAVR with SAPIEN 3/3 Ultra valves in cardiogenic shock patients had higher early mortality but similar 1-year mortality after 30 days compared to non-shock patients, with successful implantation in 97.9% of cases.
clinical_implications: TAVR is a safe and effective treatment option for aortic stenosis patients presenting with cardiogenic shock, particularly for those who survive the initial 30-day period.

title: Effectiveness of aortic valve replacement in Heyde syndrome: a meta-analysis
key_finding: Aortic valve replacement resulted in 87-92% recovery of acquired von Willebrand syndrome and 73% cessation of gastrointestinal bleeding in Heyde syndrome patients. Residual aortic valve disease was associated with lower recovery rates.
clinical_implications: Aortic valve replacement is highly effective for treating bleeding complications in Heyde syndrome, but complete valve correction is important for optimal outcomes.

title: Tricuspid Valve Academic Research Consortium Definitions for Tricuspid Regurgitation and Trial Endpoints
key_finding: This first TVARC document establishes standardized definitions for tricuspid regurgitation disease etiology, severity classification, and trial endpoints to enable meaningful comparisons between clinical trials. The document addresses gaps in understanding of TR pathophysiology and patient management.
clinical_implications: These standardized definitions will improve consistency in tricuspid valve research and clinical trials, potentially leading to better patient selection criteria and treatment outcomes.

title: Severe aortic stenosis detection by deep learning applied to echocardiography
key_finding: A deep learning model using single-view 2D echocardiography without Doppler achieved AUROC of 0.978 for detecting severe aortic stenosis in temporal validation and maintained performance (AUROC 0.942-0.952) across geographically distinct cohorts.
clinical_implications: This automated approach could enable point-of-care screening for severe aortic stenosis using handheld ultrasound devices by individuals with minimal training in resource-limited settings.

title: ESC/EACTS vs. ACC/AHA guidelines for the management of severe aortic stenosis
key_finding: The European and American guidelines for aortic stenosis management are generally congruent except for three key distinctions: intervention thresholds (LVEF 55% vs 60%), surgical bioprosthesis age recommendations (≥65 years vs multiple age categories), and different age cut-offs for TAVI vs SAVR.
clinical_implications: This comparative analysis helps clinicians understand key differences between major guidelines to inform shared decision-making in aortic stenosis management.

title: Mosaic loss of Y chromosome in monocytes is associated with lower survival after transcatheter aortic valve replacement
key_finding: Loss of Y chromosome (LOY) in blood cells >17% is an independent predictor of 3-year mortality after TAVR, with LOY monocytes showing a pro-fibrotic gene signature that enhances TGFβ signaling pathways. LOY was present in 48% of patients and associated with increased cardiac fibrosis.
clinical_implications: LOY assessment could serve as a novel risk stratification tool for men undergoing TAVR and may guide precision antifibrotic therapy approaches.

title: Healthcare resource utilisation and safety outcomes in patients undergoing transcatheter mitral valve repair - A nationwide analysis
key_finding: This appears to be a correction notice for a previously published nationwide analysis of transcatheter mitral valve repair outcomes and healthcare resource utilization.
clinical_implications: The correction ensures accurate authorship attribution for research on TMVR safety and resource utilization outcomes.

title: Neochordae implantation versus leaflet resection in mitral valve posterior leaflet prolapse and dilated left ventricle: a propensity score matching comparison with long-term follow-up
key_finding: Both neochordae implantation and leaflet resection provide excellent durability at 10 years for mitral valve repair in patients with posterior leaflet prolapse and dilated left ventricle, with similar MR recurrence rates (23.9% vs 20.8%). Neochordae implantation was associated with better left ventricular reverse remodeling and improved ejection fraction compared to leaflet resection.
clinical_implications: In patients with posterior mitral leaflet prolapse and left ventricular dilatation, neochordae implantation may be preferred over leaflet resection due to superior effects on ventricular reverse remodeling while maintaining excellent repair durability.

title: A mitral valve repair update: simple, intuitive and physiological techniques, using running sutures (part I)
key_finding: The author proposes new mitral valve repair techniques using running sutures through pathological leaflet margins that follow standard landmarks to reduce inter-surgeon variability and simplify procedures. Early experience with 8 cases showed excellent immediate outcomes with only trace or zero residual regurgitation.
clinical_implications: These standardized techniques may enhance mitral valve repair adoption by surgeons with average experience and reduce variability in outcomes through more objective, landmark-based approaches.

title: Transaxillary approach enhances postoperative recovery after mitral valve surgery
key_finding: Trans-axillary minimally invasive mitral valve surgery was associated with shorter intubation time, ICU stay, and hospital stay compared to full sternotomy, with similar perioperative morbidity and mortality rates. 30% of trans-axillary patients were discharged home versus 5% in the full sternotomy group.
clinical_implications: Trans-axillary approach provides a safe alternative to full sternotomy for mitral valve surgery with enhanced postoperative recovery and higher rates of direct home discharge.

title: Extensive aortic root endocarditis with extension into the intervalvular fibrous body: an updated report of a mitral valve sparing approach
key_finding: After radical debridement for aortic root endocarditis with mitral valve extension, mitral valve repair was feasible in 80% of patients with 18% early mortality and 51.9% reintervention-free survival at 10 years. Freedom from recurrent mitral regurgitation was 64.4% at 10 years.
clinical_implications: Mitral valve sparing approach with pericardial patch reconstruction is feasible for complex aortic root endocarditis extending to the intervalvular fibrous body, offering reasonable outcomes while avoiding mitral valve replacement.

title: The impact of annuloplasty ring or band implantation on post-repair mitral valve haemodynamic performance
key_finding: Partial band annuloplasty was associated with improved hemodynamic profile immediately after mitral valve repair compared to full rings, but this effect was short-lived with no durable impact on diastolic valve performance at follow-up.
clinical_implications: The type of annuloplasty device (partial band vs full ring) does not appear to have long-term clinical significance for post-repair mitral valve hemodynamics.

title: Medical therapy after surgical aortic valve replacement for aortic regurgitation
key_finding: Treatment with RAS inhibitors and statins was associated with reduced risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (aHR 0.71 and 0.78 respectively), while β-blocker treatment was associated with increased risk (aHR 1.35).
clinical_implications: RAS inhibitors and statins may provide beneficial secondary prevention after surgical aortic valve replacement for aortic regurgitation, while the role of β-blockers requires further investigation.

title: Ventricular Remodeling and Outcomes After Mitral Transcatheter Edge-to-Edge Repair in Heart Failure
key_finding: Left ventricular reverse remodeling at 6 months was associated with improved 2-year outcomes, but TEER treatment itself was not associated with enhanced LV remodeling compared to medical therapy alone.
clinical_implications: LV remodeling may serve as a surrogate endpoint in mitral valve trials, though TEER benefits appear independent of remodeling effects.

title: Bioprosthetic Valve Remodeling in Nonfracturable Surgical Valves: Impact on THV Expansion and Hydrodynamic Performance
key_finding: Bioprosthetic valve remodeling had limited impact on transcatheter heart valve expansion in nonfracturable surgical valves and resulted in surgical post flaring with minimal improvement in hydrodynamic function. The largest expansion gain was observed with SAPIEN 3 in 21-mm Trifecta valves (up to 12.7% increase), while the Hancock valve was less amenable to remodeling.
clinical_implications: When performing valve-in-valve TAVR in nonfracturable surgical valves, bioprosthetic valve remodeling provides limited benefit and may create unknown risks related to coronary obstruction and long-term valve function.

title: Expanding the Spectrum of TEER Suitability: Evidence From the EXPAND G4 Post Approval Study
key_finding: Patients previously deemed unsuitable for TEER based on Heart Valve Collaboratory criteria showed high rates of MR reduction (91-97% achieving ≤1+ MR) and significant functional improvement with the fourth-generation MitraClip G4 system. All groups showed functional capacity improvements and quality of life gains with low major adverse events (<3%) and low mortality rates (0-1.8%).
clinical_implications: These findings suggest that patient selection criteria for mitral TEER can be expanded beyond traditional anatomical constraints with newer generation devices.

title: Bleeding in Patients Undergoing Transfemoral Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement: Incidence, Trends, Clinical Outcomes, and Predictors
key_finding: Major bleeding within 30 days occurred in 6.6% of transfemoral TAVR patients and decreased from 11.5% in 2007-2010 to 5.5% in 2019-2022, with major bleeding associated with increased 30-day mortality (14.1% vs 4.3%) and 1-year mortality (27.8% vs 14.5%).
clinical_implications: Bleeding complications remain frequent and clinically important in TAVR patients, with major bleeding associated with sustained increased mortality risk beyond 30 days.

title: Staging Heart Failure Patients With Secondary Mitral Regurgitation Undergoing Transcatheter Edge-to-Edge Repair
key_finding: Classification of M-TEER patients into HFrEF-SMR stages (based on extramitral cardiac involvement) provided significant prognostic value, with higher stages associated with increased 2-year mortality (HR: 1.39) and less symptomatic improvement. Mortality was lowest in patients with left ventricular involvement alone (Stage 1) and highest in those with biventricular failure (Stage 4).
clinical_implications: This staging system can help clinicians better select patients for M-TEER and counsel them regarding expected outcomes based on extent of cardiac involvement beyond the mitral valve.

title: Structural Valve Deterioration After Self-Expanding Transcatheter or Surgical Aortic Valve Implantation in Patients at Intermediate or High Risk
key_finding: The 5-year cumulative incidence of structural valve deterioration was significantly lower with self-expanding TAVR compared to surgical aortic valve replacement (2.20% vs 4.38%, HR 0.46, p=0.004). This difference was most pronounced in patients with smaller annuli ≤23mm diameter.
clinical_implications: Self-expanding TAVR demonstrates superior valve durability compared to surgery at 5 years, particularly in patients with small aortic annuli, which may influence valve selection discussions in intermediate and high-risk patients.

title: Machine Learning for Dynamic and Early Prediction of Acute Kidney Injury After Cardiac Surgery
key_finding: Machine learning models using electronic medical record data achieved 0.82 ROC-AUC for predicting any AKI within 48 hours and 0.95 ROC-AUC for severe AKI, predicting AKI before clinical detection in 89% of cases.
clinical_implications: Continuous postoperative AKI risk assessment using machine learning could facilitate early interventions to limit or prevent renal injury in cardiac surgery patients.

title: Surgical outcomes of aortic valve repair for specific aortic valve cusp characteristics; retraction, calcification, and fenestration
key_finding: Aortic valve cusp retraction showed the highest odds ratio for switch to valve replacement (OR 25.14) and increased risk for reintervention, while cusp fenestration was not associated with severe aortic regurgitation recurrence or early reintervention.
clinical_implications: Cusp calcification and retraction are relative contraindications for aortic valve repair, while surgeons can successfully select appropriate candidates for repair among patients with cusp fenestration.

title: Cerebral Embolic Protection during Transcatheter Aortic-Valve Replacement
key_finding: Among patients undergoing transfemoral TAVR, cerebral embolic protection did not significantly reduce the incidence of periprocedural stroke (2.3% vs. 2.9%, P=0.30). However, disabling stroke was lower in the CEP group (0.5% vs. 1.3%).
clinical_implications: CEP use during TAVR does not significantly reduce overall periprocedural stroke risk, but may reduce disabling stroke, with the confidence interval not ruling out potential benefit.

title: Evolution and prognostic impact of cardiac damage after aortic valve replacement
key_finding: In patients undergoing aortic valve replacement, cardiac damage improved in ~15%, remained unchanged in ~60%, and worsened in ~25% at 1 year, with 1-year change in cardiac damage stage independently associated with mortality and heart failure hospitalization outcomes.
clinical_implications: Earlier detection of aortic stenosis and intervention before development of irreversible cardiac damage may improve global cardiac function and prognosis.

title: Standardized Definitions for Bioprosthetic Valve Dysfunction Following Aortic or Mitral Valve Replacement
key_finding: Expert consensus proposes standardized definitions for structural and nonstructural bioprosthetic valve dysfunction and failure that require both permanent structural leaflet changes confirmed by imaging and hemodynamic deterioration on echocardiography. Previous definitions based solely on reintervention underestimate dysfunction incidence while those based solely on high gradients overestimate it.
clinical_implications: These standardized definitions should facilitate research reporting and clinical assessment of bioprosthetic valve durability for both surgical and transcatheter valves.

title: The Effect of COVID-19 on Adult Cardiac Surgery in the United States in 717 103 Patients
key_finding: COVID-19 caused a 52.7% reduction in adult cardiac surgery volume nationally, with the Mid-Atlantic region most affected (69.7% reduction), and observed-to-expected mortality for isolated coronary bypass increased by up to 148% during the first COVID surge. Cardiac surgery volumes did not return to baseline after the first surge, indicating a persistent COVID-associated deficit.
clinical_implications: The pandemic significantly disrupted cardiac surgery delivery with increased mortality risk, suggesting need for improved surge planning and patient prioritization strategies.

title: Circulating MicroRNA-122-5p Is Associated With a Lack of Improvement in Left Ventricular Function After Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement and Regulates Viability of Cardiomyocytes Through Extracellular Vesicles
key_finding: Increased levels of circulating miR-122-5p are negatively correlated with left ventricular ejection fraction improvement after TAVR, and this microRNA regulates cardiomyocyte viability through extracellular vesicle-mediated shuttling and BCL2 suppression.
clinical_implications: Circulating miR-122-5p levels may serve as a biomarker to predict which patients will have poor LV function recovery after TAVR.

title: Surgical aortic valve replacement with a stented pericardial bioprosthesis: 5-year outcomes
key_finding: The Avalus bovine pericardial valve demonstrated 88.1% 5-year survival with no cases of structural valve deterioration and stable hemodynamic performance through 5 years. Mean gradient decreased from 42.1 mmHg at baseline to 12.5 mmHg at 5 years.
clinical_implications: This contemporary surgical bioprosthesis shows excellent 5-year outcomes with no structural deterioration, providing important data for valve selection decisions in the era of TAVR expansion.

title: Lipoprotein(a) in atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and aortic stenosis: a European Atherosclerosis Society consensus statement
key_finding: Epidemiologic and genetic studies strongly support a causal and continuous association between Lp(a) concentration and cardiovascular outcomes including aortic valve stenosis, with high Lp(a) associated with both microcalcification and macrocalcification of the aortic valve.
clinical_implications: The consensus recommends testing Lp(a) at least once in adults and implementing early intensive risk factor management for patients with elevated levels, particularly those at risk for aortic valve stenosis.

title: Effect of minimally invasive mitral valve surgery compared to sternotomy on short- and long-term outcomes: a retrospective multicentre interventional cohort study based on Netherlands Heart Registration
key_finding: Minimally invasive mitral valve surgery is as safe as sternotomy approach with no differences in 30-day mortality, 1-year mortality, or 5-year survival, but comes with a cost of reduced repair rate and more reinterventions long-term.
clinical_implications: MIMVS can be considered a safe alternative to sternotomy for mitral valve disease, though surgeons should be aware of potentially lower repair rates and higher reintervention rates.

title: Hybrid Approach Using the Cusp-Overlap Technique for Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement With a Balloon-Expandable Valve
key_finding: The cusp-overlap technique is feasible and safe for balloon-expandable SAPIEN 3 THV implantation, achieving a mean implantation depth of 3.0±1.4 mm in the CO view and 2.5±1.4 mm in the 3-cusp view. At 1-month follow-up, outcomes showed low mortality (1.0%), stroke (1.0%), and permanent pacemaker implantation (6.8%) rates.
clinical_implications: The CO technique may facilitate more accurate balloon-expandable THV positioning and potentially reduce complications like conduction blocks and permanent pacemaker requirements, especially when deep implantation needs to be avoided.

title: Outcomes of surgical aortic valve replacement over three decades
key_finding: Over 30 years (1987-2016), patient complexity increased with rising comorbidities but long-term survival after SAVR remained excellent with mean survival of 13.8 years and 20-year relative survival of 60.4% overall and 73.8% for isolated primary SAVR.
clinical_implications: These excellent long-term SAVR outcomes reinforce its role especially in younger low-risk patients and serve as a benchmark for comparing long-term TAVR results.

title: Survival after aortic root replacement with a stentless xenograft is determined by patient characteristics
key_finding: At intermediate-term follow-up, patients undergoing elective aortic root replacement with Freestyle bioprosthesis had survival comparable to the general population, while those with endocarditis and type A dissection had excess mortality. Freedom from valve reintervention was 95.0% at 5 years.
clinical_implications: Outcomes after Freestyle bioprosthesis aortic root replacement are primarily determined by patient and disease characteristics rather than prosthesis factors, supporting its use in diverse, complex aortic root pathology.

title: ST-Segment Elevation Myocardial Infarction Following Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement
key_finding: This study examines the incidence, predictors, and outcomes of ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) occurring as a complication following transcatheter aortic valve replacement procedures.
clinical_implications: This research helps identify risk factors and management strategies for STEMI complications in TAVR patients, which is important for periprocedural planning and patient care.

title: Transcatheter Replacement of Transcatheter Versus Surgically Implanted Aortic Valve Bioprostheses
key_finding: In propensity-matched cohorts, TAV-in-TAV procedures had higher procedural success rates (72.7% vs 62.4%) compared to TAV-in-SAV, with similar safety and mortality outcomes at 30 days and 1 year.
clinical_implications: These findings support the consideration of initial TAVR over SAVR in patients where life expectancy may exceed valve durability, as redo TAVR procedures appear more successful in failed transcatheter valves than in failed surgical valves.

title: Prevalence and Outcomes of Concomitant Aortic Stenosis and Cardiac Amyloidosis
key_finding: Concomitant cardiac amyloidosis in aortic stenosis patients is common (11.8%), associated with worse clinical presentation and 1-year mortality (24.5% vs 13.9%), but TAVR improves survival in AS-CA patients to similar levels as lone AS patients.
clinical_implications: TAVR should not be withheld in patients with concomitant aortic stenosis and cardiac amyloidosis as it provides survival benefit.

title: Triage and management of aortic emergencies during the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic: A consensus document supported by the American Association for Thoracic Surgery (AATS) and Asian Society for Cardiovascular and Thoracic Surgery (ASCVTS)
key_finding: A consensus algorithm was developed for triaging aortic emergency patients during COVID-19 pandemic, categorizing patients based on COVID-19 status and providing specific management protocols for COVID-positive, negative, and unknown status patients.
clinical_implications: This provides standardized approach to manage aortic emergencies during pandemic conditions while minimizing COVID-19 exposure risk to patients and healthcare providers.

title: Native Aortic Valve Disease Progression and Bioprosthetic Valve Degeneration in Patients With Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation
key_finding: Native aortic valves in TAVI patients show ongoing active disease with 18F-NaF uptake correlating with time from TAVI, and bioprosthetic valve degeneration appears comparable between TAVI and SAVR patients suggesting similar midterm durability.
clinical_implications: TAVI durability appears comparable to surgical bioprosthetic valves, and 18F-NaF PET can predict valve degeneration in both TAVI and SAVR patients.

title: Transcatheter Mitral Valve Replacement After Surgical Repair or Replacement: Comprehensive Mid-Term Evaluation of Valve-in-Valve and Valve-in-Ring Implantation from the VIVID Registry
key_finding: Significant residual mitral stenosis occurred in 8.2% of valve-in-valve and 12.0% of valve-in-ring patients, while significant residual regurgitation was more common in valve-in-ring patients (16.6% vs 3.1%), with four-year survival rates of 62.5% for valve-in-valve versus 49.5% for valve-in-ring procedures.
clinical_implications: Suboptimal hemodynamics after mitral valve-in-valve and valve-in-ring procedures are associated with need for repeat valve replacement, suggesting the importance of procedural strategies to optimize post-implantation hemodynamics.

title: Management of antithrombotic therapy in patients undergoing transcatheter aortic valve implantation: a consensus document of the ESC Working Group on Thrombosis and the European Association of Percutaneous Cardiovascular Interventions (EAPCI), in collaboration with the ESC Council on Valvular Heart Disease
key_finding: This consensus document provides updated therapeutic insights for antithrombotic treatment during and after TAVI, recommending either dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT) for 3-6 months followed by single antiplatelet therapy lifelong, or oral anticoagulation for patients with other indications for anticoagulation.
clinical_implications: Provides evidence-based guidance for clinicians on optimal antithrombotic management strategies to balance thromboembolic and bleeding risks in TAVI patients.

title: Mitral and tricuspid annuloplasty ring dehiscence: a systematic review with pooled analysis
key_finding: Overall dehiscence rate was 1.43% with significant differences by ring type: semi-rigid (1.86%), rigid (2.32%), and flexible (0.43%), with higher rates in ischemic versus non-ischemic mitral regurgitation (3.91% vs 1.63%).
clinical_implications: Rigid annuloplasty rings carry higher dehiscence risk compared to flexible rings, which should inform surgical decision-making in mitral valve repair.

title: Long-term results after aortic valve replacement for bicuspid or tricuspid valve morphology in a Swedish population
key_finding: Bicuspid morphology was independently associated with higher observed and relative long-term survival compared to tricuspid valves, with 1-, 5-, and 10-year relative survival rates of 99.0%, 97.5%, and 89.0% respectively. Adding CABG to aortic valve replacement did not affect long-term outcome.
clinical_implications: Patients with bicuspid aortic valves can expect better long-term prognosis after surgical aortic valve replacement compared to those with tricuspid morphology, providing important benchmarks for comparison with catheter-based interventions.

title: International consensus statement on nomenclature and classification of the congenital bicuspid aortic valve and its aortopathy, for clinical, surgical, interventional and research purposes
key_finding: This international consensus statement provides standardized nomenclature and classification system for congenital bicuspid aortic valves and associated aortopathy. The statement establishes uniform terminology for clinical, surgical, interventional and research applications.
clinical_implications: This consensus statement will standardize terminology and classification of bicuspid aortic valves across clinical practice and research, improving communication and consistency in management decisions.

title: Clinical impact and 'natural' course of uncorrected tricuspid regurgitation after implantation of a left ventricular assist device: an analysis of the European Registry for Patients with Mechanical Circulatory Support (EUROMACS)
key_finding: Uncorrected tricuspid regurgitation pre-LVAD and post-LVAD is associated with increased early and late mortality, but TR diminishes progressively without intervention after LVAD implantation on average. The probability of moderate-to-severe TR immediately post-implant was 10% in patients with none-to-mild TR pre-LVAD and 35% in patients with moderate-to-severe TR pre-LVAD.
clinical_implications: Patient selection for concomitant tricuspid valve surgery should not be based solely on TR grade since TR improves naturally after LVAD implantation in most patients.

title: The management of secondary mitral regurgitation in patients with heart failure: a joint position statement from the Heart Failure Association (HFA), European Association of Cardiovascular Imaging (EACVI), European Heart Rhythm Association (EHRA), and European Association of Percutaneous Cardiovascular Interventions (EAPCI) of the ESC
key_finding: Secondary mitral regurgitation in heart failure patients requires a multidisciplinary Heart Team approach with optimization of medical therapy, and while transcatheter repair has emerged as promising, current randomized trial results require careful interpretation.
clinical_implications: Provides updated practical guidance for evaluation and management of heart failure patients with secondary mitral regurgitation using a Heart Team approach.

title: Valve Academic Research Consortium 3: Updated endpoint definitions for aortic valve clinical research
key_finding: VARC-3 provides updated standardized endpoint definitions for transcatheter and surgical aortic valve clinical trials, including new definitions for bioprosthetic valve dysfunction, bleeding events, conduction disturbances, and a more granular 5-class grading scheme for paravalvular regurgitation.
clinical_implications: These standardized definitions will ensure homogeneous event reporting and accurate comparisons across aortic valve clinical research studies involving both transcatheter and surgical interventions.

title: Eight-year outcomes for patients with aortic valve stenosis at low surgical risk randomized to transcatheter vs. surgical aortic valve replacement
key_finding: At 8-year follow-up, there were no significant differences between TAVI and SAVR in the composite outcome of all-cause mortality, stroke, or myocardial infarction (54.5% vs. 54.8%). TAVI had lower structural valve deterioration than SAVR (13.9% vs. 28.3%) but similar bioprosthetic valve failure rates.
clinical_implications: These long-term results support TAVI as a durable treatment option for low-risk patients with severe aortic stenosis, with equivalent clinical outcomes to surgery at 8 years.

title: Direct Visualization of TAVR-Related Coronary Artery Management Techniques in Reanimated Beating Hearts
key_finding: The study successfully demonstrated direct endoscopic visualization of TAVR-related coronary management techniques including chimney/snorkel procedures and orthotopic snorkel stenting in reanimated beating hearts, highlighting optimal cannulation approaches in challenging scenarios.
clinical_implications: This visualization technique provides enhanced understanding of TAVR-coronary interactions and may improve training for complex coronary management during TAVR procedures.

title: 2-Year Outcomes for Transcatheter Repair in Patients With Mitral Regurgitation From the CLASP Study
key_finding: The PASCAL transcatheter mitral valve repair system demonstrated sustained favorable outcomes at 2 years with 80% survival, 84% freedom from heart failure hospitalization, MR ≤1+ in 78% of patients, and significant left ventricular reverse remodeling. Results were achieved in both functional MR (69% of patients) and degenerative MR (31% of patients).
clinical_implications: The PASCAL repair system provides a durable transcatheter treatment option for both functional and degenerative mitral regurgitation with sustained clinical benefit at 2 years.

title: Effect of Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement on Concomitant Mitral Regurgitation and Its Impact on Mortality
key_finding: In patients with moderate or greater MR at baseline undergoing TAVR, MR regressed in 44% but persisted in 56%, with MR persistence associated with higher 4-year mortality compared to MR regression (43.8% vs 35.1%).
